query: Intel Foundry 18A volume manufacturing yield rate Q4 2026 external customer tapeout status
a_issues: Content A does not directly provide the Q4 2026 volume-manufacturing yield rate or clear external-customer tapeout status. It includes weaker or speculative fragments: a forum post citing Morgan Stanley saying 18A yield is 50%, which conflicts with later-2026 reporting in the anchor; a Substack estimate about mature yields in 2027; and Intel Newsroom material about 18A-P risk production rather than the requested 18A volume-manufacturing status. It also lacks a strong source stating multiple external customers are taping out in H2/Q4 2026.
b_issues: Content B still has gaps: it does not explicitly state the anchor’s 85% yield figure, instead giving an indirect TechPowerUp/BlueFin description that yield issues are solved and HVM is sustainable. It also includes low-reliability/noisy items (LinkedIn scrape, YouTube, Reddit) and an Electronics Weekly result saying Intel has not secured meaningful external foundry customers at scale, which could confuse an LLM because that is about scale/customers, not necessarily tapeout timing. However, B’s top result does provide a clearer statement about 18A high-volume production and external offerings centered on 18A-P/18A-PT for outside customers.
reasoning: The question asks for two things: Intel Foundry 18A volume-manufacturing yield rate in Q4 2026, and external customer tapeout status. Neither set perfectly matches the anchor, but B is closer overall. Its top TechPowerUp result explicitly says Intel solved 18A yield issues and that high-volume production is now sustainable, with 30,000 wafers/month capacity, which is more directly relevant to Q4 2026 volume-manufacturing status than anything in A. It also says external customers will mainly focus on 18A-P/18A-PT and 14A, giving a usable answer on customer status. By contrast, A is muddier: it contains outdated/conflicting yield snippets (50% forum claim, Substack speculation), and the better Intel Newsroom result is about 18A-P risk production, not 18A volume manufacturing yield. A also lacks a solid result stating the external-customer tapeout status for late 2026. So while B is incomplete and somewhat noisy, it better enables an LLM to answer correctly from the provided content alone.
